CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR, age INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE orders (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER, amount DECIMAL, FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id));
INSERT INTO users (id, name, age) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 30);
INSERT INTO users (id, name, age) VALUES (2, 'Bob', 25);
INSERT INTO orders (id, user_id, amount) VALUES (1, 1, 100.50);
INSERT INTO orders (id, user_id, amount) VALUES (2, 1, 150.00);
INSERT INTO orders (id, user_id, amount) VALUES (3, 2, 200.75);
EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 20;
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 20;
EXPLAIN SELECT u.name, o.amount FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.amount > 100;
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT u.name, o.amount FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.amount > 100;
EXPLAIN SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders) AS total_orders, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users) AS total_users;
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders) AS total_orders, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users) AS total_users;
EXPLAIN SELECT u.id, (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM orders WHERE user_id = u.id) AS total_spent FROM users u;
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT u.id, (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM orders WHERE user_id = u.id) AS total_spent FROM users u;
EXPLAIN SELECT u.name, (SELECT MAX(amount) FROM orders WHERE user_id = u.id) AS max_spent FROM users u;
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT u.name, (SELECT MAX(amount) FROM orders WHERE user_id = u.id) AS max_spent FROM users u;
EXPLAIN SELECT u.id, u.name FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.id;
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT u.id, u.name FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.id;
